name: fastapi-mastery
description: Comprehensive FastAPI development skill covering REST API creation, routing, request/response handling, validation, authentication, database integration, middleware, and deployment. Use when working with FastAPI projects, building APIs, implementing CRUD operations, setting up authentication/authorization, integrating databases (SQL/NoSQL), adding middleware, handling WebSockets, or deploying FastAPI applications. Triggered by requests involving .py files with FastAPI code, API endpoint creation, Pydantic models, or FastAPI-specific features.
capabilities: Create REST APIs with path operations (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH)
Implement request validation using Pydantic models and type hints
Add authentication with JWT tokens and OAuth2
Integrate databases with SQLAlchemy and async patterns
Build WebSocket connections for real-time communication
Deploy FastAPI applications with Docker and Kubernetes
author: AbdullahMalik17
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing markdown tutorials with FastAPI code examples. No executable code, network calls, file system access, command execution, or environment variable reading. Static findings are false positives triggered by code examples in documentation being incorrectly flagged as executable patterns. The skill teaches FastAPI development patterns using placeholder credentials and example URLs for educational purposes.
valueStatement: FastAPI development requires understanding routing, validation, authentication, and async patterns. This skill provides comprehensive tutorials from beginner to advanced levels with practical code examples for building production-ready APIs.
limitations: Does not write code files to your project directory
Does not install dependencies or run development servers
Does not access external APIs or services
Does not modify your existing codebase

useCases.title: Build Your First API
useCases.description: Learn to create a basic FastAPI application with routing, query parameters, and request body handling.

useCases.title: Add Authentication
useCases.description: Implement JWT authentication, role-based access control, and secure your API endpoints.

useCases.title: Deploy to Production
useCases.description: Containerize FastAPI apps with Docker and deploy to Kubernetes with health checks.

promptTemplates.title: Create Basic Endpoint
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a basic FastAPI endpoint that returns JSON data with path and query parameters.

promptTemplates.title: Add Database
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to integrate SQLAlchemy with FastAPI including database models, session management, and async queries.

promptTemplates.title: Implement Auth
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to implement JWT authentication in FastAPI including token creation, verification, and protected routes.

promptTemplates.title: Deploy Container
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a Dockerfile for FastAPI and deploy it to Kubernetes with proper health checks.

bestPractices: Always use type hints and Pydantic models for automatic validation and documentation
Use response_model parameter to control what data is returned from endpoints
Implement dependency injection for shared logic like database sessions and authentication
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode secrets or credentials in source code - use environment variables
Do not disable validation in production - keep type checking and error responses enabled
Do not use wildcard CORS origins in production - specify exact allowed domains

faq.question: Which Python version is required for FastAPI?
faq.answer: FastAPI requires Python 3.8 or newer. Python 3.9+ recommended for using models with dict() directly.

faq.question: What are the default size limits for requests?
faq.answer: By default, FastAPI accepts bodies up to 1MB. Use max_fields, max_num_items, or custom JSON parser settings for larger payloads.

faq.question: Can I use FastAPI with async frameworks?
faq.answer: Yes. FastAPI supports async databases with SQLAlchemy 1.4+, Motor for MongoDB, and asyncpg for PostgreSQL.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: This skill only provides documentation. It does not access your files, network, or execute any code on your system.

faq.question: Why am I getting validation errors?
faq.answer: Validation errors occur when request data does not match Pydantic model types. Check field types, required vs optional fields, and nested model structures.

faq.question: How does this compare to Flask or Django?
faq.answer: FastAPI offers automatic validation, interactive docs, and native async support. Flask is simpler but requires more manual code. Django is full-stack with more overhead.
